content.problemStatement:
Given a matrix mat of size N x M where every element is either ‘O’ or ‘X’.
    Replace all ‘O’ with ‘X’ that is surrounded by ‘X’. An ‘O’ (or a set of ‘O’) is considered to be surrounded by ‘X’
    if there are ‘X’ at locations just below, just above just left, and just right of it.
constraints: Each character in the array is either 'O' or 'X'.
content.inputFormat: A 2D array of characters where each character is either 'O' or 'X'. The first two integers represent the number of rows (N) and columns (M) in the array.
content.outputFormat: The modified 2D array where all 'O' characters that are not connected to the boundary are replaced with 'X'.

hidden.label: Tricky case / empty-like / negative if allowed

solution.approach: Use DFS to mark all 'O' characters connected to the boundary, then replace all unmarked 'O' characters with 'X'.
